title: Change uptime format in `podman info` to human-readable

comments.body: Cloud podman failure is known, and the podman-in-podman tests are optional - Homu shouldn't block merge on it

:pushpin: Commit e36a5f6 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan e36a5f6e89e6c090a20e361a547c082e10214d85 -->

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/635deb63586fdfb41a7595ef667ce1546bf5f9e2.1519069243222412032/index.html)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing 635deb63586fdfb41a7595ef667ce1546bf5f9e2 to master...

Just trying to clear my overloaded inbox.  Could we tweak this from:
uptime: 359h8m21.24s
to
uptime: 359h 8m 21.24s
just adding spaces.  Adding days would be nice too if possible.  i.e.:

uptime: 14d 23h 8m 21.24s

I'm happy to play with it if you'd like.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Sure. You'd have to do a custom string format (Go provides functions to get years/weeks/days/months/seconds out of a Duration, but you'd need a lot of conditionals if we don't want to print "0 years 0 weeks 0 days...") but it shouldn't be difficult. Could be a fair bit of code in pure number of lines to handle those conditionals though.
OK, I'll give it a whack.  Should be a fun side project.
